# Part 1 of 2 — sync/matrix: sender / service / event-processor cluster

**Scope:** `matrix_message_sender.dart`, `matrix_service.dart`,
`sync_event_processor.dart` (+ its four `part` files) and all directly
assigned test files.

*Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)*

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/.../matrix_message_sender.dart` is 1200 lines — the largest impl in scope and the primary split candidate. Three independent concerns inhabit the same class:
  1. **Pre-flight / context checks + text-event dispatch** (`sendMatrixMessage`, `_ensureOriginatingHostId`, `_reconcileBundleChildEnvelope`) — ~300 lines of pure orchestration logic.
  2. **Attachment/file senders** (`_sendFile`, `_sendJournalEntityPayload`, `_sendNotificationPayload`, `_uploadAgentPayload`, `_savePayloadToDisk`) — ~400 lines of I/O helpers.
  3. **Bundle pipeline** (`_sendOutboxBundlePayload`, `_isSafeOutboxBundlePath`, bundle-child reconciliation) — ~400 lines.
  A natural split: `MatrixAttachmentSender` (file + journal + notification upload helpers) extracted into its own file, with `MatrixMessageSender` delegating to it, keeps each file well below 500 lines and makes each unit independently testable.
  **RESOLVED (adapted):** split via `part`-file extensions — `matrix_payload_senders.dart` (file/journal/notification/agent payload senders, 487 lines) and `matrix_bundle_sender.dart` (outbox-bundle pipeline, 362 lines); the sender class keeps the pre-flight orchestration and ForTesting seams at 386 lines. All moved members are library-private, so no mock delegators were needed.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../matrix_message_sender_test.dart` at 4156 lines is 3.5× the impl. The file's logical sections — context-gate unit tests (~200 lines), standalone Glados property test, journalEntity/notification/agent-payload unit tests (~2000 lines), bundle tests (~1200 lines, including the second Glados property) — should be split into at minimum `matrix_message_sender_context_test.dart`, `matrix_message_sender_payload_test.dart`, and `matrix_message_sender_bundle_test.dart`. A `_SenderTestBench` class holding `documentsDirectory`, `sender`, `room`, `sentEventRegistry`, and their stubbing would eliminate the repeated `setUp` block reproduced across groups. **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the impl split used `part` files (one library), so the test stays the single mirror under the one-test-file-per-source rule; its groups already follow the context/payload/bundle seams.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../sync_event_processor_agent_handlers_test.dart` at 3026 lines is 4.5× the 669-line impl file it covers. The file mixes basic agent entity/link scenarios (~500 lines), concurrency isolation tests (~600 lines with heavy Completer scaffolding), and outbox-bundle prefetch/cache-refresh tests (~1000 lines). Mirror-split into `sync_event_processor_agent_entity_test.dart`, `sync_event_processor_agent_link_test.dart`, and `sync_event_processor_agent_concurrency_test.dart` aligns with the stated "one test file per source file" goal once the part file is considered the unit. **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the part file IS the unit — `sync_event_processor_agent_handlers_test.dart` is already the single mirror of the `sync_event_processor_agent_handlers.dart` part; splitting it into three files would put multiple test files on one source unit, which the one-test-file-per-source rule forbids. The entity/link/concurrency/bundle concerns are separated by groups within the file.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/.../sync_event_processor_test.dart` at 2024 lines. The file covers: base processor setup, journal entity processing (~600 lines), entry link processing (~400 lines), theming selection (~300 lines), and self-echo / backfill edge-cases (~600 lines). Each group should live in its own file. The `MockJournalEntityLoader` in `sync_event_processor_test_helpers.dart` doubles as a class used by all six split files — centralizing it to `test/mocks/mocks.dart` would be the prerequisite step. **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** the library already has per-part mirrors (journal/notification/agent/outbox-bundle handler tests live next to their parts); this file is the mirror of the `sync_event_processor.dart` library shell, and a per-group split would violate the one-test-file-per-source rule. `MockJournalEntityLoader` stays in `sync_event_processor_test_helpers.dart`, which is already a shared helper file imported by the sibling mirrors.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/.../matrix_service_test.dart` at 1764 lines with 76 tests. The current file structure (basic service tests, error branches, getters, constructor paths, sendMatrixMsg variants, stats/connectivity) is already partially grouped. Splitting into `matrix_service_core_test.dart` and `matrix_service_send_test.dart` would each land under 1000 lines.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/.../sync_event_processor_agent_handlers.dart` at 669 lines. The `_fetchFromDescriptor` / in-flight-dedup infrastructure is shared between agent payload resolution and the outbox bundle resolver, making it a natural shared utility layer. Extracting it to `sync_event_processor_descriptor_cache.dart` (~100 lines) would bring the agent handlers part file to ~550 lines with a single responsibility.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** **Pervasive inline mock declarations duplicating centralized mocks.** All four test files for `MatrixService` (`matrix_service_test.dart`, `matrix_service_pipeline_test.dart`, `matrix_service_change_password_test.dart`) declare `_MockSettingsDb`, `_MockSecureStorage`, `_MockEventProcessor` / `_MockSyncEventProcessor`, `_MockQueueCoordinator` / `_MockQueuePipelineCoordinator`, etc. as private top-level classes, while `MockSettingsDb`, `MockSecureStorage`, `MockSyncEventProcessor`, `MockQueuePipelineCoordinator`, and `MockInboundQueue` already exist in `test/mocks/mocks.dart`. Rule: *never define a mock inline when it already exists in the central file.* Each inline redeclaration also loses the centralized default stubs.
  - `matrix_service_test.dart:36–68` — 14 inline private mock/fake classes
  - `matrix_service_pipeline_test.dart:27–51` — 11 inline private mock classes
  - `matrix_service_change_password_test.dart:23–39` — 9 inline private mock classes

- [x] **[HIGH]** **`MockJournalEntityLoader` declared in `sync_event_processor_test_helpers.dart` instead of `test/mocks/mocks.dart`.** Six test files import the helper; any future seventh file covering loader behavior will either need to import this private-scope file or re-declare it. Move to the centralized file now (`test_helpers.dart:26`).

- [x] **[HIGH]** **`matrix_message_sender_test.dart` inline `MockDeviceKeys` at line 32** despite `MockDeviceKeys` existing in `test/mocks/mocks.dart:32`. The inline version is identical; the only difference is the class name, which means the centralized `MockDeviceKeys` could be used directly.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`matrix_service_test.dart` `can be constructed` smoke test (line 183–187).** The test instantiates the service and asserts only `isNotNull`. Per project rules: *"No constructor smoke tests — test behavior, not existence."* The construction is already covered implicitly by every other test in the file. Remove it.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`matrix_service_test.dart` copy-paste permutations for `sendMatrixMsg` bucket variants (lines ~1077–1220).** The 14 tests from `sendMatrixMsg increments journalEntity bucket` through `sendMatrixMsg increments syncNodeProfile bucket` all follow the exact same pattern: `stubSenderSuccess()`, create a typed `SyncMessage`, call `sendMatrixMsg`, assert `messageCounts[bucket] == 1`. They should be replaced by a single parametrized helper:
  ```dart
  Future<void> _assertBucket(SyncMessage msg, String bucket) async {
    stubSenderSuccess();
    when(() => client.getRoomById(any())).thenReturn(null);
    await createService().sendMatrixMsg(msg);
    expect(service.messageCounts[bucket], 1);
  }
  ```
  with a short `for`-loop or table-driven approach.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`sync_event_processor_outbox_bundle_test.dart` directly manipulates `getIt`** (`setUp:67–73`, `tearDown:75–79`) using raw `getIt.isRegistered`, `getIt.unregister`, `getIt.registerSingleton`. Per project rules, `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()` from `test/widget_test_utils.dart` should be used, or the `getIt` dependency should be removed from `OutboxBundleUnpacker` via constructor injection of `Directory`.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`sync_event_processor_notification_handlers_test.dart` creates `NotificationsDb(inMemoryDatabase: true)` in `setUp`** of two separate groups (lines 32–35 and 218–220). The tearDown properly closes each DB. However, the construction pattern is duplicated verbatim. Extract a `_buildNotificationProcessor()` helper that returns both the `NotificationsDb` and the configured `SyncEventProcessor` to eliminate the repeated boilerplate.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`sync_event_processor_agent_handlers_test.dart` concurrency-blocker test (lines ~374–540)** uses `Completer`-based blocking (`blockerOneStarted`, `blockerOneRelease`, `blockerTwoStarted`, `blockerTwoRelease`) to prove bundle-cache isolation under concurrent `process()` calls. This is a sound technique but the Completer plumbing (~170 lines) dwarfs the 20-line assertion. Extract a `_ConcurrentBundleBlocker` test-utility class with `start()`, `awaitStarted()`, and `release()` methods to make the intent legible.

- [ ] **[LOW]** **`matrix_service_test.dart` `publishIncomingRunnerState is a no-op` test (line ~975)** asserts `expect(service.publishIncomingRunnerState, returnsNormally)`. This checks a method reference, not a call. Use `expect(() => service.publishIncomingRunnerState(), returnsNormally)`.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

The following are genuine candidates — pure logic with algebraic invariants and structured input spaces that Glados explores better than hand-written fixtures.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`_isSafeOutboxBundlePath` path-safety predicate** (`matrix_message_sender.dart:888–893`). This is a pure function: `String → bool`. A property test could generate arbitrary path strings (including traversal segments, empty components, absolute paths, null-bytes, and unicode) and verify:
  - Any path accepted by the predicate starts with `outboxBundlesSegment`
  - No accepted path contains `..` or `.` as a segment
  - Accepted paths round-trip through `p.split` without changing their first segment
  This complements the single static case in the existing bundle tests with far more input coverage.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`_ensureOriginatingHostId` dispatching** (`matrix_message_sender.dart:60–126`). The function is pure with respect to its inputs (SyncMessage variant + optional host): it dispatches on message type and fills `originatingHostId` when absent. A Glados property could generate `(SyncMessage variant, hostId: present/null)` pairs and verify: if the message family carries `originatingHostId` and it was empty/null, the returned message has `originatingHostId == host`; if already set, it is unchanged; non-carrying families are returned unchanged. Already partially covered by the existing `generated lightweight message sends` Glados test, but `SyncNotification` and `SyncOutboxBundle` dispatch arms are not in the generator's `_GeneratedSendMessageKind` enum.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`_reconcileBundleChildEnvelope` vector-clock reconciliation** (`matrix_message_sender.dart:903–1027`). This is a pure function: `(child SyncMessage, host, journalEntityById) → SyncMessage`. Key invariants to verify generatively:
  - When `messageVc` and `entityVc` differ, the returned envelope carries `entityVc` as `vectorClock`
  - Both clocks always appear in `coveredVectorClocks`
  - When `messageVc == entityVc`, no copy is made (identical object or equal content)
  - `SyncEntryLink` always has its own `vectorClock` in `coveredVectorClocks`
  - `SyncAgentEntity/Link` with null `originatingHostId` get host stamped

- [ ] **[LOW]** **`_decodeSyncEventPayload` round-trip** (`sync_event_processor.dart:73–76`). The top-level isolate entry point (`base64 → utf8 → json`) is trivially pure. A Glados property over arbitrary JSON maps would verify: `_decodeSyncEventPayload(base64.encode(utf8.encode(json.encode(m)))) == m`. Very cheap to add and locks the compute-offload path against future changes.

*No Glados candidates are identified for `_applyMessage` or `_prepareForMessage` dispatch — these functions touch Matrix I/O, DB, and file system, placing them outside the Glados scope per project guidelines.*

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** **`matrix_message_sender.dart` `_sendJournalEntityPayload` — audio/image attachment failure branch** (`lines 535–571`). The `journalAudio` and `journalImage` attachment send paths call `_sendFile` and set `attachmentsOk = attachmentsOk && sent`. If `sent == false` the method returns `null`. This path is not covered by any existing test — there is no test that simulates a file-upload failure for an audio or image attachment. The existing `sendJournalEntityPayloadForTesting` `@visibleForTesting` seam makes this straightforward to add.

- [x] **[HIGH]** **`matrix_message_sender.dart` `_sendJournalEntityPayload` — JSON-file read failure** (`lines 410–425`). The `try/catch` around `File(jsonFullPath).readAsBytes()` is not exercised by any test. Passing an invalid path (non-existent file) via `sendJournalEntityPayloadForTesting` would cover this branch.

- [x] **[HIGH]** **`matrix_message_sender.dart` outbox bundle — `gzipEncodeJson` failure path** (`lines 807–815`). The `catch` around the `gzipEncodeJson(manifest)` call logs and returns `null`. No test simulates an encode failure here (would require the gzip encoder to throw, which could be arranged by injecting a mock or wrapping).

- [x] **[HIGH]** **`matrix_message_sender.dart` `_enrichAndUploadAgentPayload` — path where both `inlineJson` and `jsonPath` are null** (`lines 1082–1089`). The method logs `'skipping ... send: missing payload and jsonPath'` and returns `null`. No test covers this terminal skip. The `@visibleForTesting` `enrichAndUploadAgentPayloadForTesting` seam exists for exactly this purpose.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`matrix_service.dart` `saveRoom` pipeline start / `queueCoordinator.triggerBridge` success log** (`lines 420–446`). The `unawaited` bootstrap that calls `pipeline.start()`, `onRoomChanged`, and `triggerBridge` is only tested in the error path (`saveRoom logs bootstrap failure when onRoomChanged throws`). A success-path test should verify that all three are called in order and the success log is emitted when the `pipeline` is set.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`matrix_service.dart` `deleteDevice` — null `deviceId` and mismatched `userId` branches** (`lines 544–580`). Three distinct error branches exist: `deviceId == null`, `config == null`, and `deviceKeys.userId != client.userID`. None are tested. The `UnsupportedError` branch for password-less deletion is also untested.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`sync_event_processor.dart` `_resolveLocalHostId` error branch** (`lines 346–360`). The `catch` around `service.getHost()` logs and assigns `null`. A test with a `MockVectorClockService` that throws on `getHost()` would cover this; the existing self-echo tests only stub a successful host lookup.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`sync_event_processor.dart` `runWithDeferredMissingEntryNudges`** (`lines 702–717`). This top-level function has two branches: `processor.runtimeType != SyncEventProcessor` (bypasses wrapper) and `sequenceLogService == null` (bypasses wrapper). The mock-processor path is covered implicitly by callers that pass a `MockSyncEventProcessor`, but the `sequenceLogService == null` path is not explicitly tested.

- [ ] **[LOW]** **`matrix_service.dart` `discoverExistingSyncRooms` getter** (`line 222`). The method delegates to `_roomManager.discoverExistingSyncRooms()`. No test verifies the delegation.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** **`matrix_service_test.dart` — 14 inline private mock classes rebuilt per test-file load.** Because Dart's `package:test` shares class loading within a shard, duplicate `_Mock*` class declarations that shadow centralized mocks add memory pressure and slow the analyzer for tooling that processes both files in the same tree walk. More concretely, each of the 76 tests in this file runs a full `setUp` that re-creates all mock instances. Switching from inline classes to their centralized equivalents would also let shared default-stub setup in `MockSettingsDb` / `MockQueuePipelineCoordinator` run once per file via `setUpAll` rather than being duplicated per-test. **Estimated impact: ~5–10% wall-time reduction across the shard containing this file.**

- [x] **[HIGH]** **`matrix_service_pipeline_test.dart` — `createService()` builds the full `MatrixService` (with all mocks) on every test call** rather than once in `setUp`. The service construction is not mutated between tests, so it could be constructed once per group in `setUp` and shared. The ~11 mock objects are also re-instantiated per test when they could be shared via `setUp`. **Estimated impact: moderate — 11 mocks × stub calls × 25+ tests.** **RESOLVED (assessed, no change):** per-test construction is the isolation-correct pattern for this repo (CI runs the whole shard in one isolate with a documented in-suite-contamination history); the entire 20-test file completes in well under a second, so shared mutable mock state is not worth the risk.

- [ ] **[MED]** **`sync_event_processor_notification_handlers_test.dart` — `NotificationsDb(inMemoryDatabase: true)` created and torn down per group** instead of per-file. If both groups ran under a single `setUp`/`tearDown` pair, the in-memory SQLite engine would be initialized once rather than twice per test run. **Estimated impact: low-moderate — SQLite initialization is non-trivial on CI.**

- [ ] **[MED]** **`sync_event_processor_outbox_bundle_test.dart` `manifest resolver on disk` group — `Directory.systemTemp.createTempSync` + `getIt.reset()` in `setUp`**, recreating a temp directory and resetting the GetIt container for every test in the group. The temp directory could be created once in `setUpAll` and cleared between tests with selective file deletion rather than full directory recreation. Similarly, a single GetIt `registerSingleton<Directory>` at the start of the group and `unregister` at the end would be more efficient than a full `getIt.reset()` per tearDown. **Estimated impact: low — but `getIt.reset()` is noted as expensive in the project guidelines for CI.**

- [ ] **[MED]** **`sync_event_processor_agent_handlers_test.dart` — the concurrency-blocker test uses unresolved `Future` chains** (two `Completer`-blocked `process()` calls running concurrently). These are real async waits by design, but the test releases the blockers sequentially with `pumpEventQueue()` gaps. The test infrastructure documentation warns that `pumpEventQueue()` inside `testWidgets` can be slow; verifying that the non-widget service test is using `async.flushMicrotasks()` or clean `await` chains instead would be worthwhile. **Estimated impact: negligible unless this causes occasional flakiness from scheduling non-determinism.**

- [ ] **[LOW]** **`matrix_message_sender_test.dart` second Glados test (`numRuns: 140`)** covers bundle scenarios with 0–8 children and 6 child kinds. The scenario space is large; `numRuns: 100` would still give strong coverage and save ~28% of the Glados CI budget for this property. Revisit after confirming no regression with reduced runs.

---

## Summary

- **3 oversized impl files** over or near 500 lines (`matrix_message_sender.dart` at 1200, `sync_event_processor.dart` at 816, `sync_event_processor_agent_handlers.dart` at 669); `matrix_message_sender.dart` is the most urgent split target.
- **3 oversized test files** over 1500 lines (`matrix_message_sender_test.dart` at 4156, `sync_event_processor_agent_handlers_test.dart` at 3026, `sync_event_processor_test.dart` at 2024); all are candidates for mirror-split along the impl's existing logical boundaries.
- **~34 inline mock class declarations** across three `MatrixService` test files that duplicate classes already in `test/mocks/mocks.dart` (`MockSettingsDb`, `MockSecureStorage`, `MockSyncEventProcessor`, `MockQueuePipelineCoordinator`, `MockInboundQueue`, etc.); violates the centralized-mocks rule consistently.
- **4 genuine Glados candidates** for pure logic currently untested generatively: `_isSafeOutboxBundlePath`, `_ensureOriginatingHostId` (additional message families), `_reconcileBundleChildEnvelope` vector-clock reconciliation, and `_decodeSyncEventPayload` round-trip.
- **6 meaningful coverage gaps**: audio/image attachment failure, JSON-file read failure, `gzipEncodeJson` failure, null-payload agent skip, `deleteDevice` error branches, and `_resolveLocalHostId` error path — all reachable via existing `@visibleForTesting` seams.
- **5 execution-speed wins**: collapsing inline mock duplication in service tests, sharing `createService()` construction in `matrix_service_pipeline_test.dart`, reducing `NotificationsDb` construction frequency, avoiding per-test `getIt.reset()` in outbox bundle tests, and trimming the second bundle Glados `numRuns` from 140 to ~100.
- **Biggest single opportunity**: split `matrix_message_sender.dart` + its 4156-line test into 3 focused pairs — this alone reduces the largest impl and its test file by ~65% each, eliminates the inline-mock duplication, and produces a `MatrixAttachmentSender` that can be unit-tested independently of Matrix SDK mock plumbing.
